error.message:
The test did not generate a result file:
============================= test session starts ============================== platform linux -- Python 3.10.12, pytest-6.2.5, py-1.10.0, pluggy-0.13.0 cachedir: /home/mscrobotics2425laptop7/openCV_ros2/build/cv_bridge/test/ament_cmake_pytest/enumerants.py/.cache rootdir: /home/mscrobotics2425laptop7/openCV_ros2/src/vision_opencv, configfile: pytest.ini plugins: launch-testing-1.0.6, ament-xmllint-0.12.11, ament-flake8-0.12.11, ament-copyright-0.12.11, launch-testing-ros-0.19.7, ament-pep257-0.12.11, ament-lint-0.12.11, cov-3.0.0, colcon-core-0.18.1
A module that was compiled using NumPy 1.x cannot be run in NumPy 2.1.3 as it may crash. To support both 1.x and 2.x versions of NumPy, modules must be compiled with NumPy 2.0. Some module may need to rebuild instead e.g. with 'pybind11>=2.12'.
If you are a user of the module, the easiest solution will be to downgrade to 'numpy<2' or try to upgrade the affected module. We expect that some modules will need time to support NumPy 2.
Traceback (most recent call last): File "/usr/lib/python3.10/runpy.py", line 196, in _run_module_as_main return _run_code(code, main_globals, None, File "/usr/lib/python3.10/runpy.py", line 86, in
